title: Vector Database (Pinecone)
description: Pinecone is a fully-managed vector database service optimized for AI/ML applications. Offers serverless architecture, automatic scaling, and low-latency similarity search. Best for: production RAG systems, semantic search, recommendation engines. Provides managed infrastructure with minimal ops overhead.

provides.description: Managed vector storage with automatic scaling
provides.reasoning: Pinecone's core capability is storing high-dimensional vectors (embeddings) for AI/ML applications. This is semantically correct and appropriately specific.
Pinecone specializes in finding similar vectors using distance metrics (cosine, euclidean, etc.). This is a fundamental capability for semantic search and RAG applications.
Pinecone supports filtering vector search results based on metadata fields, which is essential for production use cases where results need to be constrained by business logic or access control.
Pinecone is specifically a purpose-built vector database, which is more specific than just vector-storage and helps distinguish it from general embedding storage solutions.
Pinecone is a fully-managed service with automatic scaling and minimal ops overhead, eliminating infrastructure management responsibilities.
Pinecone supports immediate availability of inserted vectors for search, enabling real-time updates to the vector index without delays.

requires.reasoning: Pinecone stores and searches vectors but does not generate embeddings from raw data. It requires an external service (like OpenAI, Cohere, or custom models) to convert text/images into vector embeddings before storage.
As a cloud service, Pinecone requires API connectivity (HTTP/gRPC) to interact with the database. Applications need to make API calls to perform vector operations.

distance_metric.description: Distance metric for similarity calculation. Cosine: normalized similarity (most common for embeddings), Euclidean: absolute distance, Dot product: raw similarity score

vector_dimensions.description: Dimensionality of vectors stored in the index. Must match your embedding model output (e.g., 1536 for OpenAI text-embedding-ada-002, 768 for sentence-transformers)

index_type.description: Index deployment type. Serverless: auto-scaling, pay-per-use, no infrastructure management. Pod-based: dedicated resources, predictable performance, manual scaling

cloud_provider.description: Cloud provider for Pinecone index deployment. Choose based on data residency requirements and proximity to your application

cloud_region.description: Cloud region for index deployment. Select region closest to your application for lowest latency

pod_type.description: Pod type for pod-based indexes. p1: performance-optimized (lower latency), s1: storage-optimized (cost-effective for large datasets). Number indicates capacity multiplier

replicas.description: Number of replicas for high availability and increased query throughput. More replicas = higher availability and QPS but increased cost

service_auth_method.description: Pinecone authenticates all API requests via an API key passed in the Authorization header. Store in environment variables, never in code.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Pinecone offers a free Starter plan (1 pod, 5M vectors, 100 queries/day) suitable for development/testing. Production costs: Standard plan starts at ~$70/month (1 s1 pod, 1M vectors, unlimited queries). Enterprise production with multiple pods, higher dimensions, and replicas can reach $500-2000/month. Max represents a production deployment with 3-5 pods for high availability, handling 10-50M vectors with p2 pods ($300-500 each). Costs scale with: number of pods, pod type (s1/p1/p2), vector dimensions, and number of replicas for availability.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high complexity. Implementation requires: understanding vector embeddings and similarity search concepts (2-3 days learning), integrating with embedding models (OpenAI, Cohere, etc.), implementing indexing strategies and metadata filtering (2-3 days), building query patterns for RAG/semantic search (2-3 days), testing and optimizing query performance (1-2 days), and implementing error handling/retry logic for API calls. Team needs skills in: vector database concepts, ML/AI fundamentals for embeddings, and API integration. With AI coding assistants available for boilerplate SDK integration, complexity is reduced but domain knowledge around vector search optimization still requires 5-8 developer days (~$1500 at $200/day blended rate).
provenance.licenseCost: Pinecone is a SaaS platform with usage-based pricing, no upfront license fees. Free Starter tier available for development. Costs are consumption-based (included in monthly operational costs) with no separate licensing model. No enterprise license purchase required - pricing scales with usage through subscription tiers.

supports_nfr.reason: Pinecone standard tier provides up to 99.9% availability with multi-AZ replication as the maximum ceiling.
Vector database similarity search operations have a minimum p95 latency floor of 10ms for query processing.
Vector database query tail latency has a minimum p99 floor of 50ms under load conditions.
Pinecone managed service recovery from regional failures requires at least 5 minutes for failover.
Pinecone serverless architecture supports up to 360M vector operations per hour as throughput ceiling.
Pinecone vector database can handle up to 1000 read queries per second for similarity search workloads.
Pinecone supports up to 10000 write queries per second for vector upsert and indexing operations.
Pinecone supports optional audit logging for enterprise tiers but does not require it for all deployments.
Pinecone supports namespace-based isolation in shared indexes or dedicated indexes for tenant isolation.
Pinecone vector database can store embeddings with or without PII metadata depending on use case.
Pinecone supports GDPR-compliant deployments with data residency controls but does not enforce it by default.
Pinecone enterprise tier offers HIPAA-compliant configurations with BAA but standard tier does not require it.
Pinecone supports CCPA compliance features for California data privacy but does not mandate it universally.
Pinecone can support SOX compliance requirements through audit controls but does not enforce it by default.
Pinecone provides eventual consistency by default but supports read-your-writes consistency when configured.
Pinecone provides durable storage with multi-AZ replication and automatic backups supporting strict durability.

supports_constraints.reason: Pinecone is a cloud-agnostic SaaS service accessible from AWS, Azure, GCP, or any environment via API.
Pinecone provides official SDKs for Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Java, Go and REST API for all languages.
Pinecone is a managed database service accessible via API from any platform type including web, mobile, and ML.
Pinecone is a vector database specifically designed for vector similarity search as its primary capability.
Pattern specifically requires pinecone as the database service
Pinecone vector database works with or without additional caching layers depending on architecture requirements.
Pinecone supports both synchronous query patterns and asynchronous indexing workflows for vector operations.
Pinecone is primarily used for AI inference scenarios like RAG but can store vectors for any purpose.
Pinecone supports multi-tenant architectures through namespaces or dedicated indexes for tenant isolation.
Pinecone supports both real-time and batch vector upsert operations for bulk indexing workloads.
Pinecone does not provide distributed transaction support as a vector database focused on similarity search.
Pinecone supports real-time vector indexing and can be integrated with streaming pipelines for live updates.
Pinecone stores vector embeddings with optional metadata but is not a primary document-oriented database.
Pinecone provides key-value access to vectors by ID but is not a general-purpose key-value store.
Pinecone is a vector database, not a graph database, though it can complement graph-based architectures.
Pinecone is not a time-series database but can store timestamped vector embeddings with metadata.
Pinecone supports transactional vector upserts and queries but is not designed for traditional OLTP workloads.
Pinecone is optimized for similarity search queries, not analytical OLAP workloads or aggregations.

warn_nfr.reason: Pinecone is a managed SaaS service with operational overhead and cost. At very low query volumes (<1 req/s), simpler storage solutions may be more cost-effective.
warn_nfr.message: db-vector--pinecone: peak read QPS is {actual} req/s (<1). Managed vector database overhead may not be justified at this scale; consider simpler alternatives for low-volume workloads.
